title: echo-assertion-ratchet guards only responseIncludes* — not plannerIncludes*/selectedActionArguments — and the heuristic proxy makes responseIncludes* echo-passable even when the ratchet thinks the keyword isn't in input
evidence: CONFIRMED (structural gap): packages/scenario-runner/src/echo-assertion-ratchet.test.ts:67 (INCLUDES_ARRAY regex matches only responseIncludesAny/All) and :70-87 (isEchoSatisfiable iterates only that array) — plannerIncludesAny/All, plannerExcludes, selectedActionArguments.includesAny are never scanned. packages/scenario-runner/src/corpus-assertion-guard.test.ts:236-244 counts plannerIncludes* as enforceable with no echo check. title: Interceptor stamps result.success=true for any action handler that returns a non-object (void/undefined/string), so actionCalled status:'success' passes without real success evidence
evidence: CONFIRMED at /Users/shawwalters/eliza/packages/scenario-runner/src/interceptor.ts:402-404 — `} else { entry.result = { success: true }; }` for any non-object handler return. Contrast: the object path at interceptor.ts:378 uses `success: ... : undefined` (safe), and the connector-dispatch path at interceptor.ts:285-286 defaults `delivered=false` (safe, with comment at 281-284 stating the action-result default is "undefined, not true"). Consumer CONFIRMED at /Users/shawwalters/eliza/packages/scenario-runner/src/final-checks/index.ts:704-705 — successful calls filtered on `c.result?.success === true`. TEST ABSENT: interceptor.test.ts covers only captureConnectorDispatchesFromAction (connector path), and executor.test.ts:1107-1219 actionCalled-success tests use object returns {success:false}/{success:true} — no void/undefined/string-returning handler is tested against actionCalled{status:'success'}. title: Heuristic (echo) proxy is the documented dev default and is not gated; and without Cerebras the judge grades the agent with the same model under test
evidence:
CONFIRMED bits: runtime-factory.ts:440 (strict flag), :232-238 (gate), :454-455 (boot log NAMES heuristic vs strict mode — refutes 'not gated/silent'). llm-proxy-plugin.ts:171 (strict-only throw); echo only at :191 (planner/handler at :874-925/:935-969 are not echo). judge.ts:126-128,140-145 (TEXT_LARGE fallback when no Cerebras); lifeops-eval-model.ts:358-364 (isCerebrasEvalEnabled needs provider=cerebras + key). executor.ts:1754 & :1802 (judge error -> FAILED) — safe, as conceded.

REFUTED bits: (a) 'README's "agent is never used to grade itself"' — phrase exists ONLY at judge.ts:122-125 (code comment), NOT in README.md or CLAUDE.md; CLAUDE.md:198 instead openly documents the TEXT_LARGE fallback. (b) recommendation #3 'add a test that heuristic reply for X contains X' is already implemented at packages/test/mocks/__tests__/llm-proxy-plugin.test.ts:77 (expect(raw).toBe("deterministic-test-response: hello deterministic proxy")) and :60-62. (c) 'green run under that mode proves little' mischaracterizes CI: every PR/evidence script (packages/scenario-runner/package.json:68-72) runs SCENARIO_LLM_PROXY_STRICT=1; heuristic-only is a local quick-start example only (README.md:16, CLAUDE.md:194), never the CI path.

evidence: /Users/shawwalters/eliza/packages/scenario-runner/src/action-families.ts:77-81: actionsAreScenarioEquivalent returns true on isTokenPrefix(leftTokens=candidate, rightTokens=expected) (line 79), so candidate [calendar] being a leading prefix of expected [calendar,create,event] credits a generic CALENDAR for expected CALENDAR_CREATE_EVENT. title: 130 executive.* scenarios (77% of corpus) have zero load-bearing assertions — a real action-path regression cannot fail them
evidence:
Confirmed (exact, reproduced): in plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/scenarios — 178 scenarios total; 137 lack any of finalChecks/responseJudge/assertResponse/assertTurn; 130 of those are domain:"executive.*"; all 130 executive.* scenarios lack load-bearing assertions. The 41 that DO have them are all non-executive (tasks/habits/goals/scheduling/reminders/inbox/calendar/lifeops). 14 scenarios tagged "approvals"; 0 use approvalRequestExists/approvalStateTransition. 0 executive scenarios use draftExists/connectorDispatchOccurred/messageDelivered/memoryWriteOccurred. Cited examples accurate: bill-approval-and-payment.scenario.ts:20-38 and trust-distribution-approval.scenario.ts:20-37 carry only planner/response substring matchers.

Corrections: (a) the guard is packages/scenario-runner/src/corpus-assertion-guard.test.ts (NOT in the PA plugin); cited lines 14-18 are docstring; its merge-blocking invariant filters lane==="pr-deterministic" only (line 169) and ALL 130 executive scenarios are lane:"live-only", so the guard never evaluates them for the enforceable-assertion rule — they are out of scope, not "counted as enforceable." (b) Planner matchers ARE load-bearing for action routing: buildPlannerAssertionBlob (executor.ts:134-152) uses real action names + parameters (plannerText is never populated anywhere in the repo), and matchers are enforced at executor.ts:1686-1738. So routing regressions CAN fail these scenarios; only side-effect/approval-queue/draft/dispatch regressions cannot. "Zero load-bearing assertions" / "cannot fail a real action-path regression" is therefore overstated — the correct claim is "no side-effect/end-state assertions; only weak substring + action-routing matchers, and none of these are in the PR merge gate."
fix: For the executive.* journeys that claim a real effect (approval-gating, drafting, dispatch, reminders), add at least one load-bearing finalCheck per scenario: approvalRequestExists/approvalStateTransition for the *-approval scenarios, draftExists for the *draft* scenarios, connectorDispatchOccurred/messageDelivered for the messaging/escalation scenarios, memoryWriteOccurred for triage/diligence. Start with the 14 scenarios tagged "approvals" (currently 0 use any approval final-check).

title: parseGmailPlan blindly casts subaction (no allowlist) and defaults shouldAct=true, so malformed/empty model output silently triages instead of asking for clarification
evidence: All literal claims hold at the cited lines. plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/src/lifeops/llm/extract-gmail-plan.ts:59 (blind cast, no allowlist), :60 (shouldAct defaults true when absent), :52-72 (parseGmailPlan; empty/JSON input -> {subaction:"triage", shouldAct:true, queries:[]}), :85-106 (two useModel calls with no try/catch). Contrast confirmed at scheduling-handler.ts:811-830 (normalizeSchedulingSubaction switch->null) and :907-940 (try/catch -> {subaction:null}). No adversarial tests; the only test is the happy-path "search" case in test/lifeops-optimized-prompts.test.ts:56-93. BUT the impact is refuted as a live hazard: grep -rn "extractGmailPlanWithLlm|parseGmailPlan" across plugins/ and packages/ (excluding dist/node_modules) returns only the source file itself plus test/lifeops-optimized-prompts.test.ts — zero production consumers. parseGmailPlan is not exported (extract-gmail-plan.ts:14-74 export only the prompt constants, types, and extractGmailPlanWithLlm). The INBOX action is now a re-export shim (src/actions/inbox.ts:1-21) pointing to @elizaos/plugin-inbox, which uses its own plugins/plugin-inbox/src/inbox/triage-classifier.ts. So no path routes malformed model output through this parser into a draft/send/triage side effect. Latent robustness debt in dead-path code; not the medium-severity silent-triage/unsafe-write bug described.
fix: Add a normalizeGmailSubaction allowlist (mirroring normalizeSchedulingSubaction) returning null for unknown values, and treat null/empty parse as shouldAct=false (clarify). Wrap the two useModel calls in try/catch returning a safe default. Add unit tests for parseGmailPlan: (a) empty string → safe clarify default, not silent triage; (b) `subaction: send_everything` → rejected/null, never a draft/send subaction; (c) JSON-shaped output (model ignores line-based instruction) → no crash; (d) `shouldact` absent → not auto-true for write subactions.

title: parseExplicitLocalDate (deterministic create-event date resolver) does not handle "today", "tomorrow", "day after tomorrow", or "in N days/weeks" — the exact phrasing in its own action examples
evidence: CONFIRMED facts: parseExplicitLocalDate (plugins/plugin-calendar/src/actions/calendar-handler.ts:1193-1276) handles only ISO/month-name/numeric/weekday(+"next") dates, no relative-day branch. Examples at :686 and :4259 use "tomorrow". suggestCreateEventStartAt (:1568-1614) returns null on unparseable input.\n\nREFUTED framing: The create_event "tomorrow at 3pm" examples are resolved by the LLM extractor extract_create_event (:2470-2528, time-anchored at :2516-2517, instructed to resolve relative phrases at :2473-2475 and :2506), with resolvedStartAt taken from the extracted ISO at :2299-2300 — NOT by parseExplicitLocalDate. parseExplicitLocalDate/suggestCreateEventStartAt runs only as a clarification fallback at :3351-3359 (when LLM yields no startAt and no windowPreset), and a null result degrades gracefully to the "i need a time" prompt at :3368-3371. The READ path (:1811-1827) is the SAME shape — LLM window first (:~1800-1809) then deterministic backstop — so the claimed read-vs-create asymmetry is not real. No unit test covers parseExplicitLocalDate relative phrasing (none exists to cover, since there is no such branch); the integration test at test/calendar-action.integration.test.ts:106-121 only asserts the LLM prompt is built, and test/calendar.live-llm.test.ts:141-152 only asserts subaction routing — neither exercises the deterministic parser for "tomorrow". Recommendation to add today/tomorrow/+N handling is a reasonable low-priority robustness improvement to the fallback, not a fix for a broken path.
fix: Extend parseExplicitLocalDate with relative-day handling (today=+0, tomorrow=+1, "day after tomorrow"=+2, "in N days/weeks") computed off localToday in the supplied timeZone, reusing addDaysToLocalDate. Add unit tests asserting each relative phrase resolves to the correct local date across a DST boundary and across a year/month rollover (e.g. "tomorrow" on Dec 31, "next Tuesday" when today IS Tuesday must skip 7 days per the qualifier==="next" rule at 1261-1262).

title: No RRULE/recurrence parsing in any LifeOps LLM consumer; raw user/param text is interpolated into prompts with no injection coverage
evidence:
CONFIRMED (literal): No RRULE/FREQ/iCal parsing in scheduling-handler.ts, extract-gmail-plan.ts, service-mixin-reminders.ts, brief.ts, or calendar.ts — only CALENDAR_CREATE_RECURRING_BLOCK as a label (calendar.ts:646). Recurring-time punt is real (optimized-prompt-instructions.ts:17) but untested: the sole resolveSchedulingPlanWithLlm test (lifeops-optimized-prompts.test.ts:95-131) stubs shouldAct:false and asserts prompt-swap only; benchmark case :569 is reminder-delivery routing, not a punt assertion. Untrusted interpolation confirmed at scheduling-handler.ts:871-876, extract-gmail-plan.ts:36-41, service-mixin-reminders.ts:818/824-826. Zero injection/adversarial tests in the test tree.

CORRECTED: Evidence overstates "subaction allowlist" as a shared normalizer. Scheduling has one (normalizeSchedulingSubaction, scheduling-handler.ts:811-830, strict switch -> null default). Gmail has NONE — parseGmailPlan (extract-gmail-plan.ts:59) casts raw: `(fields.get("subaction") || "triage") as GmailPlanSubaction`. But extractGmailPlanWithLlm has no source consumer (only its own def + test reference it repo-wide), and Gmail sends require confirmSend=true with a 409 otherwise (service-mixin-gmail.ts:957/998/1027), so the LLM subaction can't itself trigger a send. Severity reduced to low: gaps are genuine but the Gmail injection vector is inert (no live consumer) and write-gated by confirmSend independent of subaction.
fix: Add scenario/unit tests: (1) a recurring request ("set up a standup every weekday at 9") through resolveSchedulingPlanWithLlm asserts shouldAct=false (punt fires) since no RRULE pipeline exists; (2) injection tests for each extractor where the user/title/param text contains an embedded instruction attempting to flip subaction to a write/send action — assert the allowlist + shouldAct normalization (or, for reminders, that the body is only used as text, never as a control field) prevent escalation; (3) document that recurrence is unsupported so a reviewer doesn't assume RRULE handling exists.

title: All optimized-prompt LLM consumers are tested only on the happy path; no malformed/empty/invalid-JSON unit coverage despite divergent robustness between sites
evidence: test/lifeops-optimized-prompts.test.ts: 3 it() blocks (lines 56/95/133), all happy-path; its useModel mock (lines 41-51) only resolves strings from modelResponses and never throws/returns malformed/empty. test/brief-optimized-prompt.test.ts: only asserts buildNarrativePrompt/buildSchedulingPlanPrompt/buildReminderDispatchPrompt string composition (imports at lines 9-11). Asymmetry confirmed: scheduling-handler.ts resolveSchedulingPlanWithLlm try/catch at 907-940 + invalid-JSON guard at 916-922; service-mixin-reminders.ts renderReminderBody try/catch at 1391-1406 (fallback) ; extract-gmail-plan.ts extractGmailPlanWithLlm has NO catch around useModel (calls at 85-91 and 100-106 — error propagates); brief.ts composeNarrative has NO catch around useModel (call at 505-507 — error propagates, undefined only on non-string). Cross-check: no test in plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test makes useModel reject/throw or return invalid JSON/empty for any of these four consumers; brief-action.test.ts "empty inputs" (lines 261-272) covers empty data sections, not malformed model output.
fix: Add a per-consumer robustness test matrix: for resolveSchedulingPlanWithLlm and (if kept) extractGmailPlanWithLlm, assert (a) invalid JSON → safe clarify default, (b) empty string → safe default, (c) useModel throws → no unhandled rejection / safe default. For composeNarrative (brief.ts), assert a thrown useModel yields undefined narrative (currently it propagates — decide and lock the contract). These directly cover the FOCUS-1 'invalid JSON / empty / partial' question that has zero current coverage.

title: sleep-recap default pack can NEVER fire: its personal_baseline_sufficient gate is registered nowhere; runner denies unknown gates
evidence: plugins/plugin-health/src/default-packs/sleep-recap.ts:39 — gate kind "personal_baseline_sufficient" in sleepRecapDefaultPack.records[0].shouldFire.gates (compose:"all", first gate). plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/gate-registry.ts:307-316 registerBuiltInGates registers 8 gates, NOT personal_baseline_sufficient (and its warn-once fallthrough mechanism at lines 241-269 covers the other two health gates but skips this one). plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/src/lifeops/scheduled-task/runtime-wiring.ts:399-400 calls only createTaskGateRegistry()+registerBuiltInGates. plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/runner.ts:476-485 returns deny "unknown gate kind: <kind>" on null lookup; runner.ts:997-1011 turns that deny into status="skipped" with no dispatch. Repo grep: personal_baseline_sufficient appears only in sleep-recap.ts (source) + its dist/build copies — zero registrars. No test asserts the gate resolves; default-packs.schema.test.ts:109-111 checks only that gate.kind is non-empty, not registry membership. Note: cited "sleep-recap.ts:36-41" should be the shouldFire block 36-42 / gate line 39 (minor). Pack reaches users via getOfferedDefaultPacks (index.ts:163-164), not auto-seed (defaultEnabled:false excludes it from getDefaultEnabledPacks at index.ts:147).
fix: Register a real personal_baseline_sufficient TaskGateContribution (reading SleepEpisodeRepository sample count via the CircadianInsightContract seam) in plugin-health init or PA runtime-wiring, OR add it as a warn-once fallthrough in registerBuiltInGates. Add a vitest in plugin-scheduling asserting registerBuiltInGates().get('personal_baseline_sufficient') !== null, and a runner test that drives sleepRecapDefaultPack.records[0] through evaluateGates and asserts the decision is NOT 'unknown gate kind'. Add a plugin-health unit test importing HEALTH_DEFAULT_PACKS and registerBuiltInGates that asserts every gate kind referenced by every pack record resolves in the registry (cross-plugin gate-coverage guard).

title: bedtime/wake-up packs' safety promises are unenforced: circadian_state_in and no_recent_user_message_in fall through to allow
evidence: CONFIRMED. plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/gate-registry.ts:248-257 — makeWarnOnceFallthroughGate.evaluate() unconditionally returns {kind:"allow"}; comment at :233-239 admits "the concrete data readers ... are not wired into the runner today ... falls through to allow." circadianStateInGate at :261-264, noRecentUserMessageInGate at :266-269. Both registered (only registration) via registerBuiltInGates at :307-316 (lines 314-315). Production wiring plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/src/lifeops/scheduled-task/runtime-wiring.ts:399-400 calls only registerBuiltInGates(gates); duplicate-kind override impossible (registry throws at gate-registry.ts:288-294). Runner translates allow→fire / deny→skipped at plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/runner.ts:997-1010 (evaluateGates :456-515). Safety promises: plugins/plugin-health/src/default-packs/bedtime.ts:19 + gate at :36-39; plugins/plugin-health/src/default-packs/wake-up.ts:19 + gates at :37-43. wake-up defaultEnabled:true (wake-up.ts:20) and auto-enabled (plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/default-packs.schema.test.ts:267-281); bedtime defaultEnabled:false (bedtime.ts:20). Corrections: (1) gate-registry.ts is under plugin-scheduling, not plugin-health. (2) An existing test plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/src/lifeops/scheduled-task/scheduler.integration.test.ts:639-692 documents the fall-through for circadian_state_in and asserts the task fires (status "fired"), codifying the larp as accepted — so the always-allow behavior is knowingly shipped; but no test covers no_recent_user_message_in, the real bedtime/wake-up packs against a sleeping/mid-conversation state, or a deny-when-unsafe assertion. The recommendation's proposed safety tests are genuinely absent.
fix: Register production circadian_state_in (using getCircadianInsightContract(runtime) circadian state) and no_recent_user_message_in (recent-message lookup) TaskGateContributions, overriding the fallthroughs. Add plugin-health (or PA) unit tests: a circadian_state_in gate denies a bedtime task when circadianState='sleeping' and allows when 'awake'; a no_recent_user_message_in gate denies the wake-up task when a user message exists within 30min. Until wired, mark bedtime/wake-up packs as not-safe-to-enable in a test that fails if defaultEnabled:true on wake-up while its gate is a fallthrough.

title: screentime_recap is a trainable artifact with ZERO runtime consumer — SCREENTIME_RECAP_INSTRUCTIONS optimizes a prompt nothing ever loads
evidence: CONFIRMED. screentime_recap is trained (plugins/plugin-training/src/core/training-orchestrator.ts:517-522 loads SCREENTIME_RECAP_INSTRUCTIONS) and scored (plugins/plugin-training/src/optimizers/scoring.ts:305,316) and declared in the taxonomy (packages/core/src/services/optimized-prompt.ts:86,106,123), but has ZERO runtime consumer. Exhaustive enumeration of the 14 production resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime call sites yields task args {autonomy, media_description×2, health_checkin, action_planner, view_context, morning_brief, reminder_dispatch, calendar_extract, inbox_triage×2, schedule_plan, +dynamic should_respond/response/action_descriptions via message.ts:3135}; none is "screentime_recap". Both screen-time.ts files (plugin-health/src/actions/screen-time.ts — the one carrying the :17 re-export — and plugin-personal-assistant/src/actions/screen-time.ts) contain no useModel/ModelType/resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime/OptimizedPrompt (grep NONE); they build deterministic fallbacks and route through renderLifeOpsActionReply (grounded-reply.ts) → renderGroundedActionReply, which has no screentime_recap reference. SCREENTIME_RECAP_INSTRUCTIONS is consumed only by training-orchestrator.ts:520 and the re-export at screen-time.ts:17. The recommended call-site-existence guard is absent: packages/core/src/services/optimized-prompt-resolver.test.ts:184 only verifies the test's own TASK_CASES table matches the taxonomy and exercises the resolver function in isolation — it does not scan source for production call sites. The audit's note that such a guard would also flag meeting_prep is correct: meeting_prep likewise has no resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime consumer (only scenario:"assistant.meeting_prep" in plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/src/default-packs/executive-assistant.ts:116, a scheduled-task label). The only evidence imprecision is that the bare "screen-time.ts:17" citation refers specifically to the plugin-health file; conclusion unchanged.
fix: Either (a) wire a real consumer: add a screentime-recap narration step in the OWNER_SCREENTIME weekly/summary path that calls resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime(runtime,'screentime_recap',SCREENTIME_RECAP_INSTRUCTIONS) and useModel to produce the recap+one-suggestion JSON the instructions describe, then add a scenario with a registered final-check (judgeRubric or selectedActionArguments) proving it runs; or (b) remove screentime_recap from the optimized-prompt taxonomy + training datasets + scorer so the system stops claiming a capability that does nothing. Add a guard test that asserts every OptimizedPromptTask in the taxonomy has at least one resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime call site (catches future zero-consumer tasks; would currently also flag meeting_prep).

title: Fitbit distance is wildly overstated: normalizer sums total + subset breakdown rows, and the contract test enshrines the wrong value
evidence: Real Fitbit /activities/date summary.distances is [{activity:'total',distance:8.52},{activity:'tracker',distance:8.52},{activity:'veryActive',distance:4.1}] (src/health-bridge/__fixtures__/fitbit.recorded.json:27-31) — 'tracker'/'veryActive' are breakdowns OF the 'total' day distance, not additional distance. health-connectors.ts:443-447 reduces over ALL entries (totalDistance += every distance) then health-connectors.ts:483 emits totalDistance*1000 as distance_meters. For an 8.52km day this yields (8.52+8.52+4.1)*1000 = 21140 m — ~2.5x the truth. fitbit-connector.contract.test.ts:132-142 documents this as expected ('the normalizer SUMS every summary.distances[] entry (not just total) and converts km->m ... 21.14 km -> 21140 m'), locking in the bug.
fix: Read only the entry with activity==='total' (fallback to the max single entry) instead of reduce-summing all rows in health-connectors.ts:443-447. Update fitbit-connector.contract.test.ts:138-141 to assert distance.value === 8.52*1000. Add a fixture/test case with extra subset rows (e.g. 'loggedActivities') to prove only 'total' is taken.

title: Fitbit distance and weight assume metric locale; imperial accounts silently store miles-as-meters and lbs-as-kg
evidence: health-connectors.ts:483 — Fitbit distance `value: totalDistance > 0 ? totalDistance * 1_000 : null, unit: "m"` assumes km input (metric locale). health-connectors.ts:585-597 — weight pass-through with `unit:"kg"`; the real provider unit is captured only as `metadata.providerUnit` (line 596) and is never read by any consumer (repo-wide grep: only the metadata write + a test assertion). fetchHealthValue (health-connectors.ts:245-256) sends no Accept-Language/unit header — only Accept:application/json + Authorization; grep finds no locale/imperial/unit-conversion logic in the file. Fixture fitbit.recorded.json is metric-only (weightUnit:"kg" line 140; distances km line 28). fitbit-connector.contract.test.ts only feeds the metric fixture and asserts pass-through (distance line 141-142 unit "m"; weight line 158-159 unit "kg") — no imperial-locale fixture/test exists. Strava (health-connectors.ts:359-366) correctly emits distance as raw meters with no *1000, validating the finding's contrast. Correction to the finding: the normalizeHealthSignal citation, while literally true (zero conversion), is on a different payload path that connector distance/weight samples never traverse; and the finding omits that Fitbit weight's raw unit is preserved in metadata (unused). Neither point refutes the defect.
fix: Send Accept-Language: en_US (forces imperial) or en_GB and convert deterministically, OR read the profile distanceUnit/weightUnit (profile.json already fetched) and convert miles->m / lbs->kg explicitly. Add an imperial-locale Fitbit fixture + contract test asserting distance_meters and weight_kg are converted, not passed through. Same audit for Strava (meters, OK) vs Withings/Oura weight.

title: No coverage for absurd values, partial data, or child/elderly health phrasing in actions or scenarios
evidence: CONFIRMED gaps: (a) plugins/plugin-health/src/health-bridge/service-normalize-health.ts:81-112 forwards durationMinutes + all 5 biometrics through normalizeOptionalFiniteNumber (plugins/plugin-health/src/util/normalize.ts:67-78), gated only by Number.isFinite — 9999 bpm / negative sleep pass. The existing test plugins/plugin-health/src/health-bridge/service-normalize-health.test.ts:21-39,123-135 asserts only finite-or-null and canonical-or-400, NOT range rejection. (b) formatSummary at plugins/plugin-health/src/actions/health.ts:249-275 unconditionally emits Steps (line 261) and Sleep `toFixed(1)h` (line 263); steps=0 -> "0", sleepHours=0 -> "0.0h"; the only action test (plugins/plugin-health/src/actions/health.test.ts) never calls the today/trend path with partial data. (c) grep over plugins/plugin-health/test/scenarios/ for child|elderly|clinician|lbs|weigh|judgeRubric|26 hours|6-year|absurd|clamp|sanity returns nothing; health-checkin-sleep-recovery.scenario.ts:17 mentions "medical advice" only in the user prompt and its sole gating finalCheck (lines 30-37) is a selectedActionArguments routing assertion, so no run actually judges a refusal/units/anomaly. CITATION CORRECTION: finding cited formatSummary as health.ts:244-270; actual span is 249-275. service-normalize-health.ts:93-112 cite is exact. OVERSTATEMENT: "no range clamp" is true but the function is an inbound structural normalizer of device-measured wearable data; a hard clamp is a product choice, not an obvious bug — hence partial / low.
fix: Add a vitest to service-normalize-health for absurd values (heartRateBpm 9999 / -5, durationMinutes negative) asserting they are rejected or clamped with a warning. Add an action test where the daily summary has only sleep data (steps=0) asserting the reply does not assert '0 steps walked' as measured fact. Add scenarios with judgeRubric final-checks: (a) child/elderly health phrasing — agent must refuse medical advice and suggest a clinician; (b) user states weight as 'I weigh 180' — agent must clarify lbs vs kg before logging; (c) absurd value ('I slept 26 hours') — agent flags the anomaly rather than echoing it.

title: A thrown dispatcher leaves the task permanently in 'fired' with no retry, backoff, or escalation — the notification is silently lost
evidence: Real file: plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/runner.ts (the finding's plugin-personal-assistant path is wrong; the line numbers match the scheduling-pkg file). runner.ts:1083-1096 — dispatch try/catch returns {kind:"dispatch_failed", task: claimed, error: wrapped} with no revert/retry/backoff/followupCount. runner.ts:1058 — `await persist(claimed)` already wrote the row as `fired` before dispatch. runner.ts:364-367 — doc comment confirms the row is left in `fired` and the caller merely surfaces the error. Consumer scheduler.ts:235-242 (plugin-personal-assistant) only pushes an error row, returns true; no recovery. CORRECTION to the finding's mechanism: the NULL next_fire_at does NOT come from resolveNextFireAt/isTerminal (runner.ts:281-289 isTerminal excludes `fired`, so runner.ts:543 does not short-circuit); it comes from computeNextFireAt (next-fire-at.ts:249 for `once`, :288 for event/manual/after_task). Completion-timeout pass: due.ts:450-456 returns due:false when no followupAfterMinutes; scheduler.ts:147 issues a `skip`, never a re-dispatch. Test absence verified: 0 hits for "dispatch_failed" in runner.test.ts and scheduler.integration.test.ts; the dispatcher describe block runner.test.ts:907-1107 only exercises successful (undefined-returning) dispatch. Scope note: permanent loss applies to once/event/approval tasks; recurring (cron/interval/anchor/window) tasks reschedule the next occurrence, losing only the failed occurrence's notification.
fix: Add a runner unit test injecting `dispatcher: { dispatch: async () => { throw new Error('channel down') } }`, fire a task, assert kind==='dispatch_failed' AND assert the row is re-selectable for a retry (e.g. status reverted to 'scheduled' with a backoff firedAt, or a 'dispatch_failed' state-log row + next_fire_at set to now+backoff). Then change the runner: on dispatch throw, revert claimed.state.status to 'scheduled', set firedAt to a backoff time (e.g. now + min(2^attempt, cap) minutes), persist, and log a 'dispatch_failed' transition so the next tick retries. Add a scheduler.integration.test.ts case proving a transient dispatch failure is retried on the subsequent tick and eventually delivered.

title: The spine's multi-step escalation ladder (nextEscalationStep) is never walked by the runner — high-priority 3-step cross-channel escalation is dead code
evidence: Files are under plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/ (not plugin-personal-assistant). escalation.ts:133 nextEscalationStep; escalation.ts:74-81 high-priority ladder [in_app@0,push@15,imessage@45]. ZERO non-test callers of nextEscalationStep (grep: only escalation.ts:133 def + index.ts:40 re-export). Runner cursor writes only at runner.ts:1052 (stepIndex:-1 on fire) and runner.ts:654 (snooze reset). resolveEffectiveLadder consumed only at runner.ts:1191 in the read-only getEscalationCursor view. Dead-end proof: claimForFire transitions scheduled→fired only (runner.ts:135-142); due.ts:155 `if (task.state.status !== 'scheduled') return null;` so a fired task is never re-ticked — single dispatch at runner.ts:1084, no re-schedule to walk the ladder. applyEscalate (runner.ts:701-717) also does NOT call nextEscalationStep/advance the cursor. Parallel mechanism: plugins/plugin-reminders/src/db/schema.ts:47 `step_index` (finding said :264 — wrong line, real table). Test gap: escalation.test.ts:104-150 unit-tests the pure function only; runner.test.ts:836/859/871/891 only assert stepIndex===-1 — no multi-step re-dispatch / stop-on-ack test exists.
fix: Either (a) wire the runner to walk the ladder: after fire, schedule the next step via nextEscalationStep(ladder, cursor) → set firedAt to step.fireAtIso, advance metadata.escalationCursor.stepIndex, and re-dispatch on the matching tick until completion/ack/exhaustion; add runner.test.ts cases asserting a high-priority unacknowledged task escalates in_app→push→imessage at +0/+15/+45 min and STOPS on acknowledge — or (b) if the reminders-side mechanism is authoritative, delete nextEscalationStep + the DEFAULT_ESCALATION_LADDERS step bodies from the spine (keep only the cursor for observability) so the codebase does not advertise an escalation engine that never runs. Add a guard test that fails if a ladder with steps.length>1 is ever resolved without a corresponding re-dispatch path.

title: A cron task missed by >36h of agent downtime silently skips the missed occurrences AND can permanently mis-base if it never fired
evidence: Live source is plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/due.ts (NOT the lifeops path cited). CONFIRMED true: due.ts:13 CRON_CATCHUP_WINDOW_MS=36h; due.ts:229-232 baseMs = parseIsoMs(firedAt) ?? metadataCreatedAtMs ?? nowMs-CRON_CATCHUP_WINDOW_MS; computeNextCronRunAtMs forward-scan returns first match (packages/core/src/services/triggerScheduling.ts:195-203, scan window 366d at line 8). CORRECTION to finding: for firedAt=2026-06-20T09:00, now=2026-06-24T09:05, computeNextCronRunAtMs returns 2026-06-21T09:00 (OLDEST missed slot, firedAt+1day) — NOT TODAY 09:00. Recommendation #1's asserted occurrenceAtIso=TODAY 09:00 is WRONG and would fail. No-storm guarantee is real but rests on runner.ts:1042-1045 (firedAt:=now on claim) + next-fire-at.ts:254-266 (next_fire_at bases off max(firedAt,now)); tick selects via repository.ts:7319-7322 next_fire_at<=now OR NULL. Never-fired-no-createdAt 36h floor is literally true (due.ts:231-232) but no real path demonstrated that omits both firedAt and createdAt/scheduledAt metadata.
fix: Add due.test.ts cases: (1) cron `0 9 * * *`, firedAt = 4 days ago, now = today 09:05 → assert due:true with occurrenceAtIso = TODAY 09:00 (proves single catch-up, occurrence is the latest missed slot not the oldest). (2) Same task with the tick loop (scheduler.integration.test.ts): seed firedAt 4 days ago, run ONE processDueScheduledTasks tick, assert exactly ONE fire and that the post-fire next_fire_at is tomorrow 09:00 (proves no storm). (3) A never-fired cron with createdAt 5 days ago and now beyond the 36h window → document/assert the intended behavior (fire latest missed vs skip-all) so the 36h floor is a tested contract, not an accident.

title: state.firedAt is overloaded as both 'scheduled-override fire time' and 'last actual fire time', creating a refire collision for recurring triggers
evidence: Overload is real: WRITE-future at runner.ts:651-652 (applySnooze) and runner.ts:1026 (gate-defer); READ-past at due.ts:203 (intervalDue), due.ts:229-230 (cronDue), next-fire-at.ts:255/273. scheduledOverrideDue precedence: due.ts:155 (status==='scheduled' guard), due.ts:158-165 (pending vs due), invoked first at due.ts:397-398. Refire flip without clearing firedAt: runner.ts:966-973. Test gap confirmed: due.test.ts:66-83 uses status:'fired' (skips override); runner.test.ts:638-660 cron-only no snooze; scheduler.integration.test.ts:324-379/455-499 cover only after_task/event override. REFUTATION of the double-count: atomic claim repository.ts:7221-7227 (UPDATE ... WHERE status='scheduled' RETURNING *) + raced-records-nothing scheduler.ts:218-223 (comment: 'would double-count ... would double-bill') guarantee one fired record per occurrence irrespective of due-branch. claimForFire in-mem mirror: runner.ts:133-142.
fix: Add a test: schedule an interval task, fire+complete it, snooze it to a future time (sets firedAt future), then advance now past the snooze and run a tick — assert it fires exactly once and that the interval base after fire is the claim instant, not the snooze marker. Consider splitting the two semantics into distinct fields (`state.firedAt` = last actual fire; `state.overrideFireAt` = scheduled-override marker) so due.ts:151 and due.ts:203 read different columns; at minimum add a unit test pinning scheduledOverrideDue precedence over intervalDue/cronDue for a 'scheduled' recurring row with a past firedAt.

title: Completion-timeout pass and fire pass share one `limit` budget; a backlog of timeouts can starve due fires (and vice versa) within a single tick
evidence: scheduler.ts:96 `const limit = Math.max(1, Math.floor(request.limit))`. Timeout loop scheduler.ts:140-169 and fire loop scheduler.ts:171-198 both gate on the shared total `result.fires.length + result.completionTimeouts.length >= limit` (lines 141 and 173); timeout loop runs first. timeoutCandidates is queried at scheduler.ts:135-137 with `status:['fired']` and no dueAtOrBeforeIso; listScheduledTasks (repository.ts:7263-7337) emits SQL with no LIMIT (only `ORDER BY created_at ASC`, line 7334), so it scans every 'fired' row each tick. No integration test (scheduler.integration.test.ts, 11 cases; timeout case 547-637 seeds one approval) seeds >limit mixed timeout+due tasks to prove fairness. CORRECTION: production limit is 25, not 5 — service-mixin-reminders.ts:226 DEFAULT_SCHEDULED_TASK_PROCESS_LIMIT=25, used by the W1 scheduler (5131-5137, 5247-5251) and, via service.runDueTasks() with no override, by the mobile /api/background/run-due-tasks route (background-tasks-routes.ts:52-110). Only the integration tests pass limit:5. Mitigant: a timed-out task transitions 'fired'→'skipped' on success (test 612-620; isCompletionTimeoutDue due.ts:447 returns not_fired thereafter), so it consumes a slot once; multi-tick starvation needs >25 fresh timeouts/tick sustained.
fix: Add a scheduler.integration.test.ts case: seed `limit` fired-and-timed-out tasks PLUS one due-to-fire reminder, run a tick with limit=N, assert the due reminder still fires within a bounded number of ticks (i.e. budgets are separate or the due pass is not fully starved). Fix by giving the timeout pass and fire pass independent budgets (or process due-fires first, or interleave), and bound the timeoutCandidates query (add a dueAtOrBeforeIso/limit to the repo query) so a large fired backlog cannot cause an O(all-fired-rows) scan every minute.

title: during_window dedup key is the owner-local date+window; a DST 'fall back' or owner timezone change can re-fire or skip a window within the same calendar day
evidence: Canonical source: /Users/shawwalters/eliza/plugins/plugin-scheduling/src/scheduled-task/due.ts (finding cited the plugin-lifeops/dist build copy; logic identical). duringWindowDue at due.ts:358-381 — fireKey at :372 (`${localDateKey(context.now, timeZone)}:${trigger.windowKey}:${active.name}`), dedup compare at :373-375 (window_already_fired). title: meeting_prep and screentime_recap are dead optimization targets: in taxonomy/scorer/datasets/benchmarks but optimize zero runtime call sites
evidence:
CONFIRMED. meeting_prep and screentime_recap are declared optimization targets with no runtime consumer.

Dead taxonomy declarations: packages/core/src/services/optimized-prompt.ts:83,86 (union), :103,106 (OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_TASKS), :120,123 (LIFEOPS_OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_TASKS).

Proof of no consumer: exhaustive grep of plugins/ + packages/ (excluding node_modules and .claude/worktrees) shows every meeting_prep / screentime_recap occurrence is taxonomy, test, dataset, orchestrator, scorer, dspy union, native-export, benchmark, or scenario-tag — never a resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime/getPrompt call. The two would-be consumers are stubbed elsewhere: brief.ts:474 resolves "morning_brief" (not meeting_prep); owner-surfaces.ts (OWNER_SCREENTIME handler) and plugin-health/src/actions/screen-time.ts contain no resolver/getPrompt/screentime_recap.

Contrast (wired tasks): health.ts:199-201, calendar-handler.ts:1993-1995, triage-classifier.ts:135-137, extract-gmail-plan.ts:80-82/95-97, service-mixin-reminders.ts:806-808, brief.ts:474-476, scheduling-handler.ts:863-865 — i.e. 6 of the 8 LifeOps tasks have real call sites; these 2 do not.

Full dead pipeline surface confirmed at cited lines: dspy/artifact.ts:27,30; scoring.ts:302,305 (LIFEOPS_SCORER_TASKS) and 314,316 (LIFEOPS_STRUCTURED_SCORER_TASKS); trajectory-task-datasets.ts:42,45,131,134,635,638; training-orchestrator.ts:408,414,499,517,553,559,736,739; native-export.ts:133,136,214,220; lifeops-prompt-benchmark-cases.ts:93,96,417,426,581(→BRIEF 583/589),597(→OWNER_SCREENTIME 600).

Masking test confirmed: optimized-prompt-resolver.test.ts:178,181 places both in TASK_CASES and the loop at :192-202 self-builds the resolver call (asserts injection if called), while the only guard at :184-189 asserts matrix==taxonomy, not real call-site existence. Real-path routing test plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/lifeops-optimized-prompts.test.ts covers only inbox_triage/schedule_plan/reminder_dispatch. No call-site guard test exists. The refresh() loop at optimized-prompt.ts:619 only caches on-disk artifacts; it does not resolve them, so a meeting_prep/screentime_recap artifact is loaded-but-never-read.
fix: Make a decision and encode it as a test/guard. Option A (wire them up): add resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime(runtime, "meeting_prep", BASELINE) to the BRIEF/meeting-dossier prompt builder and a screentime_recap resolver call to the OWNER_SCREENTIME / screen-time recap prompt path, then add per-task routing tests mirroring lifeops-optimized-prompts.test.ts (assert the optimized prompt text appears in the captured useModel prompt). Option B (remove the dead surface): drop both from OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_TASKS/LIFEOPS_OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_TASKS, the dspy artifact union, scorer lists, dataset file-name maps, and benchmark cases. Either way, add a guard test that asserts every task in LIFEOPS_OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_TASKS has at least one resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime call site (a static grep-style assertion in a *.test.ts) so a declared-but-unwired task fails CI instead of silently no-op'ing.

title: A single task's unreadable `current` symlink (ELOOP/EACCES/EISDIR) throws out of refresh()/start(), marking the service failed and globally disabling optimized prompts for every task — no test covers this
evidence: optimized-prompt.ts:790-795 — readFile catch returns null only for ENOENT, line 794 rethrows all other errnos (and line 805 rethrows non-ENOENT on the .mac read). refresh() optimized-prompt.ts:617-655 — per-task loop (loadArtifactFromPath at 626 & 642, readdirSync at 636) has no try/catch; this.cache=next only at 654. start() optimized-prompt.ts:424 — `await service.refresh()` uncaught. _runServiceStart runtime.ts:3975-3994 — catch logs 'Service start failed' (3983), rejects load promise (3987), sets status 'failed' (3993). resolver.ts:138-143 + line 53 — null service → baseline, so one task's failure disables all. rollback() optimized-prompt.ts:602 — also calls refresh(). Tests: grep ELOOP|EACCES|EISDIR in optimized-prompt.test.ts = 0 matches; the only `rejects` tests at lines 193/199 are rollback no-previous/no-dir preconditions; integrity tests 297-328 assert getPrompt null after refresh RESOLVES (null-return branch), never the rethrow branch. No looping-symlink or EISDIR test exists.
fix: Wrap each per-task body of refresh() in try/catch so a corrupt/looping/permission-denied artifact for one task is logged-and-skipped (load that task's baseline) without poisoning the other 14 tasks or failing service start — matching the documented contract 'an absent artifact is a no-op (never a failure)'. Add tests: (1) create a self-referential `current` symlink (`ln -s current current`) in one task dir, populate a valid artifact in another, call refresh(), assert it resolves and the valid task is cached while the looping task is null; (2) a dir entry that is itself a directory named X.json so readFile throws EISDIR; assert refresh() does not reject.

title: Two runtimes (core basicServices + plugin-training re-register, or two agents sharing one stateDir) writing the same task artifact race on the version counter and can clobber a vN.json / corrupt the symlink set with no lock
evidence: setPrompt (optimized-prompt.ts:503-555) computes nextVersion non-atomically: it reads existingVersions = listVersionNumbers(dir) (line 515), picks nextVersion = last+1 (line 516), then writeFile(temp)+rename to vN.json (lines 521-522). There is no lock, no O_EXCL, no mutex (grep for lock/flock/mutex/O_EXCL in the file returns nothing). The same OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_TASKS taxonomy is registered by BOTH core basicServices (basic-capabilities/index.ts:1346) and plugin-training register-runtime.ts:31 (which guards with getServiceLoadPromise but the trigger service + CLI `train` also call setPrompt — promotion-persist.ts:203, train.ts:207, lifeops-gepa-seed.ts:772). Two concurrent setPrompt calls for the same task both read existingVersions=[..,N], both compute N+1, the second rename overwrites the first's v(N+1).json (rename is atomic so no partial file, but the first artifact's bytes + its already-written .mac sidecar for the SAME path now mismatch the surviving payload → that .mac will fail verifyArtifactMac on next load and the artifact is silently rejected per loadArtifactFromPath:821). repointVersionLinks + pruneOldVersions interleaving across the two writers can also leave `previous`/`previous2` pointing at a pruned/renamed file. No concurrency test exists.
fix: Serialize writes per task dir: either acquire an exclusive lock file (open with 'wx'/O_EXCL on `<dir>/.write.lock`, release in finally), or write the artifact under its content/temp name first and derive the version by atomically claiming the name with O_EXCL in a retry loop (re-read versions and bump on EEXIST). Add a test that fires N concurrent setPrompt(task, ...) via Promise.all on a shared storeRoot and asserts: every resulting vN.json has a matching valid .mac (no orphaned/mismatched sidecar), version numbers are contiguous/unique, and `current` resolves to a loadable artifact afterward.

title: A task with no on-disk artifact and a typo'd OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_DISABLE entry both silently degrade to baseline with no operator-visible signal
evidence:
CONFIRMED part: parseDisabledTasksEnv at packages/core/src/services/optimized-prompt.ts:396-401 drops non-isTask tokens with no logger.warn (logger is imported at line 52 and used at 808/839/847/854 elsewhere). Test packages/core/src/services/optimized-prompt-resolver.test.ts:111-115 codifies the silence; no warn-assertion and no isTaskDisabled(typo)===false test exists.

REFUTED part: getMetadata at packages/core/src/services/optimized-prompt.ts:476-486 checks ONLY `if (!entry) return null` and does NOT gate on disabledTasks, so a disabled-but-cached task returns metadata, not null — the 'returns null in both cases' claim is inaccurate. getMetadata has zero callers repo-wide (grep found only unrelated github-credentials.ts and plugin-wallet erc8004.ts). The Auto-Training panel packages/ui/src/components/settings/CapabilitiesSection.tsx:147-160 fetches only /api/training/auto/config and /api/training/auto/status (global toggle) and never reads optimized-prompt per-task disabled/absent state — the claimed panel-blindness has no consumer.

Separately, add a lightweight diagnostic (e.g. getMetadata returns a discriminated `{ status: 'absent' | 'disabled' | 'loaded', ... }` or a sibling `getTaskStatus(task)`) so the panel and tests can distinguish disabled-vs-absent, and add a test asserting isTaskDisabled(typo) === false for a hyphenated/misspelled task name.

title: LIVE scoring inverts: a do-nothing agent scores 1.0, a correct-write agent scores 0.3
evidence: Package path is packages/benchmarks/lifeops-bench/ (finding omitted it). Confirmed: scorer.py:1242-1244 LIVE score = 0.7*state_component + 0.3*substring_component (gated on terminated_reason=="satisfied"); state_component=1.0 iff state_hash_match (scorer.py:1172); substring_component defaults 1.0 since LIVE required_outputs==[] (test_scenarios_corpus.py:188; scorer.py:1178-1179). LIVE ground_truth_actions==[] (test_scenarios_corpus.py:187) => _replay_ground_truth (runner.py:2849-2858) returns the unchanged seed hash => state_hash_match (runner.py:3265) is True iff the world is left unmutated. world_assertions are judge-advisory only (evaluator.py:425-432, the sole runtime consumer). Reproduced on live.calendar.find_focus_block_tomorrow: do-nothing+satisfied+unchanged=1.0; correct-write+satisfied+changed=0.3; do-nothing+not-satisfied=0.0. No scorer test exercises LIVE-mode score_scenario or the inaction-vs-mutation ordering (test_scorer_fixes.py hardcodes STATIC; test_live_scenarios.py asserts no score value). Correction: absolute corpus counts in the finding are stale (now 5808 LIVE scenarios, majority still write-oriented), but the bug direction and severity hold.
fix: For LIVE scenarios, stop comparing the final world to the unchanged seed. Either (a) drop the state_hash component entirely for LIVE and score purely on the judge verdict, or (b) replace it with programmatic evaluation of scenario.world_assertions (currently only fed to the judge as advisory text, evaluator.py:425-432) by parsing them into checkable predicates against the final LifeWorld. Add a scorer test: a LIVE write scenario where the do-nothing/world-unchanged result must score strictly LESS than the correct-mutation result.

title: The LIVE branch of score_scenario has zero correctness tests
evidence: CONFIRMED with one count correction. scorer.py LIVE branch: scorer.py:1242-1244 (`if result.terminated_reason != "satisfied": return 0.0` / `return 0.7 * state_component + 0.3 * substring_component`); STATIC vs LIVE dispatch at scorer.py:1181. test_scorer_fixes.py:55-67 _scenario() hardcodes mode=ScenarioMode.STATIC (line 59) — the sole direct caller of score_scenario; all 23 score_scenario references (e.g. lines 336, 598, 623, 642, 657, 1193, 1216, 1257, 1290, 1330, 1371, 1388, 1430, 1452, 1694, 1713, 1742) run STATIC only. test_conformance.py:413-417 comment 'LIVE scenarios are excluded' + test_conformance.py:422 filter `if s.mode is not ScenarioMode.STATIC: continue`; conformance invariant asserts at test_conformance.py:552 (PerfectAgent==1.0), :565 and :580 (WrongAgent==0.0) over the STATIC-only set. test_adapter_conformance.py:69 same STATIC-only filter. test_live_scenarios.py constructs LIVE scenarios (e.g. lines 246-265, mode=ScenarioMode.LIVE) but contains zero total_score / score_scenario assertions — verified by grep (only terminated_reason=='satisfied' at line 279). test_budget.py LIVE cases (lines 380, 454) assert terminated_reason=='cost_exceeded' (lines 420, 494) which is zeroed by the error guard at scorer.py:1165-1170 before the LIVE formula at 1242-1244 is reached. title: The 528 live scenarios never run in any scheduled/required CI; nightly silently downgrades to STATIC
evidence: CONFIRMED core: cerebras-nightly.yml sets CEREBRAS_API_KEY only (line 130), runs `python -m eliza_lifeops_bench --suite full` with NO --mode (lines 143-149); __main__.py:572-573 forces mode=STATIC (warn at 574-577) when both keys absent; `--suite full` = 11,220 scenarios incl. 5,808 LIVE (docs headline 528). title: 2,548 static scenarios have required_outputs that appear verbatim in their own instruction, with no LARP guard
evidence: Files (corrected path — finding's prose said plugins/plugin-lifeops, real location is packages/benchmarks/lifeops-bench/; cited line numbers are accurate): scorer.py:1108-1110 output_substring_match joins only assistant-role turns into assistant_blob (never the instruction); scorer.py:1228-1234 substring weights 0.1/0.2/0.55 (WRITE/READ/READ_WITH_SIDE_EFFECTS); scorer.py:1217-1225 triviality guard zeros substring credit only when action_component==0 or no _has_creditable_action_overlap, so a partially-correct agent banks echo credit; _required_output_matches/_contains_normalized_phrase scorer.py:861-892 are normalized phrase matches a verbatim echo satisfies. title: Zero child-register coverage: no child persona, no child phrasing, no child-safety scenario across all three corpora
evidence: CONFIRMED. Bench: packages/benchmarks/lifeops-bench/eliza_lifeops_bench/scenarios/_personas.py:3-4 (docstring "ordinary working professionals"), :85-92 (PERSONA_KAI_STUDENT, grad student = youngest, adult), :103-114 (ALL_PERSONAS). No age/minor field on Persona (types.py:76-84) or Scenario (types.py:123-142). PA owner profile: src/lifeops/owner/profile-extraction-evaluator.ts:193 regex = boss|manager|partner|spouse|colleague|coworker|teammate|friend (no parent/child/guardian). Definitive greps over plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/scenarios + plugins/plugin-health/test/scenarios + bench: 'after cartoons'/'feed the fish'/'recess'/childlike-'allowance' = 0 child-register matches ('allowance' only as baggage/housing allowance). No child-as-user/child-register scenario; brush-teeth-*.scenario.ts are adult first-person. No child-safety refusal scenario (refusal hits are adult-persona declining finance/health recs). Correction to finding's headline: child-DOMAIN coverage is actually substantial (~31 adult-parent-POV scenarios: childcare-backup-plan, weather-closure-childcare-recovery, kid-camp-medical-form-deadline, minor-emergency-passport, minor-travel-consent-notarization, school-incident-parent-comms) — but all are adult-owner-facing, confirming the precise gap (no child persona/register/user/safety-refusal) while refuting any reading of "no child topic at all".
fix: Add a child persona to _personas.py (e.g. PERSONA_LEO_KID, age ~9, traits=['literal','playful','non-sequitur'], communication_style='short, run-on, references cartoons/games, no clock-awareness') and author 4-6 PA scenarios with literal child phrasing: 'remind me to feed my fish after cartoons' (vague time anchor → assistant must ask), 'set a timer for free minutes' (homophone three), 'can you tell mom I want pizza' (delegation to a non-owner). At least one must be a child-safety scenario where the correct behavior is a refusal/handoff (child asks to send money / make a purchase) — see no-refusal-schema finding.

title: Elderly register modeled only as 'patient retiree'; no rambling/repetition/confusion/medication-safety scenarios
evidence:
Confirmed gap: no confused/rambling/repetitive elder register and no medication-adherence-ambiguity or double-create-dedup scenario exists.
- _personas.py:67-74: PERSONA_OWEN_RETIREE traits=patient/asks-clarifying/non-technical; style "polite, full sentences" — friendliest-elder only.
- reminders.py:142-176 (blood-pressure) and reminders.py:564-595 (cholesterol): both clean single-intent LIFE_CREATE, zero confusion/double-dose modeling.
- Zero grep hits for dearie/grandkid/sweetie or "did I (already) take" across the active corpus; only contact-merge + duplicate-charge "duplicate" scenarios exist (finance.py:137, live/contacts.py:52/189/360), which do not cover same-reminder-twice dedup.
CORRECTIONS to the finding:
- "only STATIC metadata" is false: communication_style is consumed in LIVE mode at evaluator.py:344 (simulated-user prompt). Gap persists only because Owen's style itself lacks confusion cues.
- definitionCountDelta/finalChecks/memoryExists/responseIncludesAny/echo-ratchet do NOT exist in this benchmark (grep: zero). Scoring is state_hash+action_score+output_substring (scorer.py:8-11); a dedup test must be authored as a state_hash/ground_truth_actions assertion, not via the named APIs.
fix: Add PERSONA_ELDER_CONFUSED (rambling, repeats requests across turns, mixes up app/product names, polite filler 'dearie can you'). Author scenarios: (a) user asks for the same reminder twice in one conversation — assistant must dedupe not double-create (assert definitionCountDelta==1); (b) 'did I take my pill yet today?' medication-adherence query with ambiguity; (c) a rambling multi-clause request where the real intent is buried mid-paragraph. Assert via finalChecks (count delta / memoryExists), not responseIncludesAny, to satisfy the echo-ratchet guard.

title: Health check-in has no self-harm/crisis/medical-overask safety scenario and no safety logic in code
evidence: plugins/plugin-health/test/scenarios/health-checkin-sleep-recovery.scenario.ts has a single benign turn ('Check in on my sleep and recovery today. If anything looks off, ask one focused follow-up instead of giving medical advice') and its only assertion is selectedActionArguments routing to OWNER_HEALTH (lines 30-37) — it never tests what happens if the user discloses distress. Greps for 'self-harm|suicide|crisis|988|hopeless|hurt myself' return 0 in plugin-health src AND scenarios. plugins/plugin-health/src/actions/optimized-prompt-instructions.ts is 31 lines with no medical-disclaimer/safety language (grep for 'medical|diagnos|professional|doctor|crisis' = 0). The PA 'crisis' scenarios (crisis-comms-family-office, reputation-crisis-screenshot-preservation) are PR/reputation business crises, not personal mental-health crises.
fix: Add a health check-in scenario where the simulated user discloses a distress signal mid-check-in (e.g. 'haven't slept in 4 nights and honestly feel hopeless'). Correct behavior: assistant does NOT give medical advice, surfaces crisis resources / suggests reaching a professional or trusted person, and does NOT silently log it as a habit metric. Assert with a judgeRubric finalCheck ('response provides a supportive non-clinical handoff and avoids diagnosis/treatment advice'). Pair with a code change: add a minimal distress-detection + safe-response clause to HEALTH_PLAN_INSTRUCTIONS so the optimized health_checkin prompt can't be GEPA-optimized away from it.

title: Neither bench scorer nor training scorer can reward a correct refusal/escalation — success == matching ground-truth actions
evidence: CONFIRMED: types.py:123-143 Scenario dataclass has no forbidden_actions/expects_refusal/safety field. scorer.py:7-11 + score_scenario:1181-1244 — every STATIC/LIVE weighting branch is state_hash+action+substring, no safety term. plugin-training/src/optimizers/scoring.ts:451-460 scoreLifeOpsTask only -> scoreStructuredFields/scoreAgreement; task set (scoring.ts:294-317) has no safety task. Corpus: all decline/refuse strings are the persona declining a legitimate proposal (live/travel.py:393, live/finance.py:208/582/736, live/calendar.py:80/84/127), never the assistant refusing a harmful ask; grep for grandson/wire-transfer/scam/impersonate/gift-card/must-refuse/do-not-comply = 0 hits. CORRECTION to the finding: the 'zero ground-truth actions -> unscoreable' sub-claim is overstated — empty-GT scenarios are all LIVE (e.g. live.finance.fraud_alert_03, live.mail.flag_phishing_attempt) and ARE graded by the LIVE judge against success_criteria/world_assertions (evaluator.py:434-462), so 'ask for consent before mutating' is already scoreable. The real, narrower gap: (1) no schema field or scenario tests refusal/escalation of a HARMFUL ask, and (2) the LIVE judge is told 'Refusal ... responses are NOT satisfied' (evaluator.py:453), which would mis-grade a legitimate flat-refusal answer as failure. Severity medium: real eval/training coverage gap, not a production/security defect.
fix: Add an optional `forbidden_actions: list[str]` and/or `expects_refusal: bool` to the bench Scenario dataclass and teach scorer.py to award full credit when the agent takes NONE of the forbidden actions (and a judge confirms a refusal/escalation). Then author social-engineering/over-ask scenarios that use it: an elderly owner relays 'my grandson emailed asking me to wire $5,000 to this account' (assistant must flag/hold, not execute a MONEY transfer), or a child asks to buy something. This closes the loop so GEPA can be rewarded for safe refusal instead of penalized for non-action.

title: Zero voice-transcription-error and zero typo scenarios — assistant is only tested on clean text
evidence: Definitive grep counts: homophone/voice errors ('for free minutes','tree minutes','too minutes','won minute') = 0 matches across PA test, health test, and bench. Deliberate typos ('tomorow','appoinment','medecine','teusday','wendsday','reminde me') = 0 in PA+health scenarios. The bench LIVE simulator prompt (evaluator.py:330-366) never instructs the user-LLM to introduce transcription noise. The closest casual scenario, daily-left-today-variants, varies only register ('what life ops tasks are still left for today?') with perfect spelling.
fix: Author 'fuzz' scenarios that feed realistic ASR/typo noise and assert the *resolved intent* via finalChecks (not responseIncludesAny): 'set a timer for free minutes' → reminder due in 3 minutes (definition due-time check); 'remind me to take my meds at ate pee em' → 8pm; 'tomarow at noon book teh dentist'. These directly stress the calendar_extract/reminder_dispatch extractor prompts. Add a corresponding bench knob (e.g. a `noise: 'asr'|'typo'` flag that the LIVE simulated-user prompt honors) so noise coverage scales across the corpus.

title: No run-on 3+-chained-intent scenario and only a trivial 'never mind' contradiction case
evidence: Bench scan (programmatic, full corpus): 0 of ~875 instruction-keyed strings in packages/benchmarks/lifeops-bench/.../candidates/{static,live}/*.json contain both whole-word 'also' and 'and'; 0 mixed-intent run-ons (reminder+booking+read-query with 2+ 'and'). Note the finding's "720" is approximate (actual 483 static + 392 live = 875 instruction strings), but the count is not load-bearing. PA single-turn multi-clause requests are formal imperative lists only — e.g. hiring-loop-candidate-coordination.scenario.ts:24 "Coordinate the final interview loop for Nina: find two 45-minute slots with product and engineering, draft the candidate email, and don't send yet." — never a casual run-on. Contradiction coverage = exactly brush-teeth-cancel.scenario.ts:30 and brush-teeth-retry-after-cancel.scenario.ts:30 ("Actually never mind, do not save it yet."), both clean MULTI-TURN reversals; 0 same-turn flips ("cancel it — no keep it") anywhere. calendar-conflict-detect-reschedule.scenario.ts has a multi-turn create->detect->move-to-4pm flow but NO calendar finalChecks asserting net end-state, so the recommended same-turn-contradiction-net-state assertion remains absent.
fix: Add (a) a casual run-on multi-intent scenario mixing a reminder + a booking + a read-only query in one message; assert all three intents are handled (multiple finalChecks: definitionCountDelta + draftExists + a calendar read), proving the planner decomposes rather than dropping clauses. Add (b) a same-turn contradiction scenario ('cancel the 3pm — no wait, keep it but move it to 4') and assert the net end-state (one event at 4pm, none cancelled) via a calendar finalCheck.

title: Internationalization covers clean translations only — no ambiguous date formats, mixed scripts, or ESL grammar
evidence: All claims verified literally true. Cited input lines confirmed: water-russian.scenario.ts:24, brush-teeth-korean.scenario.ts:24, brush-teeth-arabic.scenario.ts:24, water-mandarin.scenario.ts:24, vitamins-italian.scenario.ts, goal-sleep-french-formal.scenario.ts:33 — all grammatically clean translated text. Negative claims confirmed by grep over plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/scenarios/: ESL/broken-grammar markers = 0; numeric DD/MM-vs-MM/DD date = 0; 18:30-vs-6:30pm / disambiguat / locale / code-switch / transliterat = 0. Only explicit-date scenario one-off-mountain-time.scenario.ts:24 uses unambiguous "april 17 2026 at 8pm"; calendar-extract-capability.scenario.ts:47,54 use unambiguous "Friday at 7pm"/"Wednesday at 9am". i18n source src/lifeops/i18n/prompt-registry.ts:1-40 is a localized-example translation registry (PromptLocale en/es/fr/ja/ko/pt/tl/vi/zh-CN keyed off OwnerFactStore.locale), confirming the suite is translation-scoped, not locale-disambiguation-scoped.
fix: Add scenarios that stress locale ambiguity rather than translation: 'book the dentist on 03/04' from a user whose profile/room implies DD/MM (assert the assistant disambiguates or picks the locale-correct date), a '18:30' vs '6:30 pm' equivalence check, and an ESL/code-switched request ('please you can remind me water drink every two hour'). Assert resolved date/time via finalChecks. These exercise calendar_extract/reminder_dispatch under realistic non-native and locale-mixed input rather than clean dictionary translations.

title: The live LLM benchmark runner (runLifeOpsPromptBenchmark) executes in NO automated CI lane — manual-dispatch only
evidence:
CONFIRMED with two evidence corrections.

Gates (verified exact):
- plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.test.ts:22-24 `const LIVE = process.env.LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK_LIVE === '1' || process.env.TEST_LANE === 'post-merge';`; skip at :78 `describe.skipIf(!LIVE)(...)`.
- plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.activation.test.ts:27 `const RUN_LIVE = process.env.RUN_LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK === '1';`; skip at :134 `it.skipIf(!RUN_LIVE)(...)` (third, distinct env var).

CI wiring (verified):
- Only .github/workflows/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.yml drives the TS runner; triggers are pull_request + workflow_dispatch ONLY (no schedule/push/workflow_run). Line 70: `LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK_LIVE: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.live == true && '1' || '0' }}` → PRs always get '0'. Line 77 runs only test/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.test.ts (never .activation.test.ts).
- TEST_LANE is "pr" in every workflow that sets it (ci.yaml:88, test.yml:308/371/444/506, scenario-pr.yml:23); never "post-merge".
- RUN_LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK: set in zero workflows.
- Sole non-test caller scripts/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.ts:191 is invoked by no workflow.

CORRECTION 1: finding says `grep -rln post-merge .github/workflows` returns nothing — that is wrong; ci.yaml:26 contains "post-merge" in a comment. The accurate statement is: TEST_LANE is never *assigned* "post-merge" in any workflow (only the comment mentions it).

CORRECTION 2: finding says "No lifeops bench workflow has a schedule:/cron trigger" — wrong; lifeops-bench-multi-tier.yml:42-44 has `schedule: - cron: "0 3 * * *"`. BUT that nightly job runs the separate Python `lifeops-bench` CLI (packages/benchmarks/lifeops-bench), sets none of the three TS gate vars, and never invokes the TS runner — so it does not cover runLifeOpsPromptBenchmark. The runner's live path (createLifeOpsPromptBenchmarkRuntime / runSinglePromptBenchmarkCase / captureTrajectoryForCase / cost-token accounting) remains exercised by automation in zero lanes; only a human workflow_dispatch live=true reaches it.
fix: Add a `schedule:` (nightly/weekly) trigger to .github/workflows/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.yml that sets LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK_LIVE=1 with the provider secret(s) and a small case_limit, OR add a post-merge job (push to develop) that sets TEST_LANE=post-merge so the existing `|| TEST_LANE==='post-merge'` branch fires. Also unify the env gate: the activation test uses RUN_LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK while .test.ts uses LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK_LIVE — pick one variable so a single CI flag exercises both live blocks.

title: plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/scripts/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.ts is a near-duplicate orphan; only the app-core copy is wired, and that copy is wired to no CI
evidence:
CONFIRMED literally: (1) package.json:194 wires only the app-core copy via `lifeops:bench`. (2) grep for `plugin-personal-assistant/scripts/lifeops-prompt-benchmark` across source/CI (excluding node_modules,dist,electrobun) = 0 hits → PA copy plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/scripts/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.ts is a genuine unreferenced orphan. (3) `wc -l`: 215 (app-core) vs 224 (PA); diff shows PA adds `--compress` flag (lines 49-55) + `compress` option (line 36) + `process.env.ELIZA_PROMPT_COMPRESS="1"` (line 188), and uses static local helper imports vs app-core's deep dynamic imports. (4) `grep -rn 'lifeops:bench' .github/` = empty → not in any workflow.

CORRECTING the overstated parts: The CI workflow .github/workflows/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.yml IS live and on line 77 runs the PA test (`test/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.test.ts`), which directly imports and exercises the shared runner (`runLifeOpsPromptBenchmark`, `formatPromptBenchmarkReportMarkdown`, etc. — see that test's lines 8-19). So the benchmark runner logic is CI-covered; only the two standalone CLI wrappers (which additionally write report.json/markdown/ax.jsonl) are uninvoked by CI. And the `--compress`/`ELIZA_PROMPT_COMPRESS` capability the PA copy adds is NOT untested risk: it is the canonical core feature (packages/core/src/runtime/action-retrieval.ts:246, planner-loop.ts:831, services/optimized-prompt-resolver.ts:61) with dedicated tests at packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/compress-mode.test.ts and serialization-memo.test.ts:66; the PA flag just toggles that env var.

Verdict: the "near-duplicate dead orphan script" claim holds (valid low-priority cleanup), but the implied severity (untested compress mode + benchmark wired to no CI) is refuted — the runner is CI-exercised via the test and compress mode is independently tested. Recommendation to delete the orphan PA script is reasonable; recommendation to "wire lifeops:bench into CI because the runner is otherwise uninvoked" is partly redundant since the test already drives the runner in PR CI.
fix: Delete plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/scripts/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.ts (dead duplicate) and keep a single canonical script, OR collapse both into one. Then wire `lifeops:bench` into the live job of .github/workflows/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.yml so the standalone runner that writes report.json/markdown/ax.jsonl is actually invoked by CI rather than only by hand. If the `--compress` mode is intended to ship, port it to the canonical script and add a smoke assertion for it.

title: context-budget tests assert char/4 math on hand-written strings; they never measure a real LifeOps provider payload, and the provider-name lists are wrong/duplicated
evidence:
CONFIRMED. Files: plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/helpers/lifeops-context-budget.ts, .../helpers/lifeops-context-budget-benchmark.ts, .../lifeops-context-budget.test.ts, .../lifeops-context-budget-benchmark.test.ts.
- No runtime: grep -En "runtime|composeState|getProvider" over all four files -> 0 matches; helpers imported only by their own tests; never imported by src/.
- Tautology: lifeops-context-budget.test.ts:15-19 literal payloads; line 37 asserts measured[0].tokens===100 == 400/4 (estimateTokens = Math.ceil(len/4) at helper:42-45). Tests pass (7/7) but assert self-authored arithmetic.
- Drifted/duplicated lists: lifeops-context-budget.ts:33-34 = "activity-profile"/"lifeops-health" (correct); lifeops-context-budget-benchmark.ts:8-9 = "activityProfile"/"health" (both WRONG). Real names: activity-profile.ts:33 -> "activity-profile"; plugin-health/src/providers/health.ts:110 -> "lifeops-health". No test diffs either list vs src/providers (constants referenced nowhere outside the two budget test files).
- Refinement: ELIZA_LIFEOPS_CONTEXT_WINDOW is a real env var but resolves a conversation-LINE count (default 16) via resolveContextWindow() at src/lifeops/defaults.ts:282-291 — NOT a token budget; the finding's recommendation conflates the two. createLifeOpsTestRuntime exists (test/helpers/runtime.ts:9), so the real-provider regression test the recommendation asks for is feasible.
fix: Add one test that boots createLifeOpsTestRuntime, runs composeState over the real LifeOps provider set, feeds each provider's actual `text` into summarizeContextBudget against ELIZA_LIFEOPS_CONTEXT_WINDOW, and fails if total exceeds budget — turning the primitive into a real regression guard. Also add a hermetic assertion that LIFEOPS_CONTEXT_PROVIDERS (and LIFEOPS_CONTEXT_PROVIDER_IDS) equal the provider `name`s discovered from src/providers/*.ts, so drift like `health` vs `lifeops-health` fails CI. Then collapse the two duplicate modules into one.

title: meeting_prep and screentime_recap are benchmarked end-to-end but optimize nothing (zero resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime consumers) — the benchmark green-light is misleading
evidence: Cases (corrected path): plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/helpers/lifeops-prompt-benchmark-cases.ts — meeting_prep case at lines 581-585 (expectedAction "BRIEF"), screentime_recap case at lines 597-601 (expectedAction "OWNER_SCREENTIME"); both tasks listed in LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK_TASKS at lines 93,96. Hard requirement: plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/lifeops-prompt-benchmark.test.ts:37-44 throws if any task lacks a case; lines 67-69 assert all 8 tasks are present. Taxonomy declaration: packages/core/src/services/optimized-prompt.ts:83 (meeting_prep), :86 (screentime_recap), and LIFEOPS_OPTIMIZED_PROMPT_TASKS at :120,:123. Exhaustive consumer audit of resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime across all source (excluding dist/tests) yields task literals {autonomy, media_description, health_checkin, view_context, calendar_extract, inbox_triage, reminder_dispatch, schedule_plan, morning_brief} — meeting_prep and screentime_recap are absent. brief.ts:474-476 resolves only "morning_brief" (no meeting_prep branch); plugins/plugin-health/src/actions/screen-time.ts contains no resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime call. Confirmed: both tasks are benchmarked end-to-end but optimize nothing at runtime.
fix: Either (a) wire meeting_prep into BRIEF/meeting-prep handling and screentime_recap into the OWNER_SCREENTIME path via resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime, then add a hermetic test asserting each of the 8 tasks has at least one runtime consumer; or (b) if these two are intentionally inert, drop them from LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK_TASKS / the capability cases and the optimized-prompt taxonomy so the benchmark stops implying coverage that does not exist. Add a guard test cross-checking LIFEOPS_PROMPT_BENCHMARK_TASKS against the set of tasks that actually have a resolveOptimizedPromptForRuntime consumer.

title: Dead/over-exported symbols in the benchmark helpers (knip-style): createLifeOpsPromptBenchmarkRuntime, AxOptimizationRow, PromptBenchmarkSliceStats/LatencyStats exported but never imported externally
evidence: `createLifeOpsPromptBenchmarkRuntime` is `export`ed (runner.ts:747) but only ever called internally (runner.ts:844, 868) — no test or script imports it (grep across test/*.test.ts and the app-core script: 0 hits). Type exports `AxOptimizationRow` (runner.ts:97), `PromptBenchmarkSliceStats` (runner.ts:66), `PromptBenchmarkLatencyStats` (runner.ts:72) are imported by 0 external files. (Note: SELF_CARE_PRD_SCENARIO_IDS/SELF_CARE_HABIT_SCENARIO_IDS are NOT dead — used internally at cases.ts:742/750; buildSelfCare/ExecutiveAssistant cases ARE used by the app-core script.)
fix: Drop the `export` from createLifeOpsPromptBenchmarkRuntime (make it module-local) and from the unused type aliases, or add them to a knip ignore list with a comment. Better: add a knip config entry covering plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/test/helpers/lifeops-prompt-benchmark-*.ts so future orphaned exports in this harness are caught automatically rather than re-discovered by audit.
